intent: Complete the provenance ladder's top rung: make real_owned_stakes (the only grade that flips is_t4_utility) EARNABLE not assertable, with HARD operator independence — an unbacked/self-dealing claim must never fabricate a T4 utility signal.

Implement.integration: core/helix_adoption_trial.py (_effective_grade now verifies real_owned_stakes by attestation hash; aggregate downgrades unbacked claims)

Verify.result: 679 tests OK (663 -> 679); helix_validate PASS; fidelity path regression clean

invariants_held: HARD independence: operator == wedge author is REJECTED (dogfooding disqualifies real_owned_stakes) — deliberate contrast to fidelity where dogfooding is allowed+flagged
real work only: simulated / decision_count<=0 / bad ledger head rejected; bound to a real ledger head sha256
objective outcomes: at least one integer measure + replay_verified True; sentiment-only rejected
only VERIFIED real_owned_stakes flips is_t4_utility; aggregate downgrades unbacked claims with an explicit 'cannot fabricate a T4 utility signal' problem
single operator = utility_candidate, NOT T4 pass — full T4 verdict still needs the multi-participant pilot gate (docs/PILOT-PROTOCOL.md)
ladder_status: provenance ladder COMPLETE in code/contract: simulated_unverified (done) + fidelity_attested (done, 1 real receipt) + real_owned_stakes (done, unforgeable, awaiting a real independent operator). No rung can now be asserted without verifiable backing.
ceiling: This layer makes the T4-eligible grade unforgeable; it does NOT produce real_owned_stakes data. That requires an independent party running the wedge on real work with owned outcomes — a real-world event (P5_5 external pilot), not autonomous code.
next: USER-DECISION: (a) commit this owned-stakes layer, (b) P5_5 external pilot (the real-world event that GENERATES real_owned_stakes data this layer will honestly judge)
